behavior_summary: When the gargoyle object is examined (U8P_SGARGL::look event), the script builds the label string "gargoyle " and asks the item/process to 'bark' that string to the viewer.
The handler operates on a process/object pointer passed in (inferred from BP+06h) and does not modify any global flags or persistent game state.

After calling Item::bark the code frees the temporary string, inspects the returned value via an implies-style operation, then suspends/returns — suggesting the return may influence engine control flow or UI but no further local state is changed.
notable_control_flow: Entry on U8P_SGARGL::look event -> prepare temporary string "gargoyle "
Push process/object pointer (BP+06h) and string, call external Item::bark
Consume Item::bark return value with an implies/pop sequence
open_questions: Is the parameter at BP+06h strictly a process ID/pointer or a broader object descriptor? (inferred as object/process pointer)
What semantics does the consumed return value from Item::bark carry for the engine (visual vs control flow cue)?
Does the implies/pop sequence trigger any conditional behavior elsewhere in the engine that depends on the bark return code?

main_function.name: U8P_SGARGL::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the 'U8P_SGARGL::look' (examine) event for a gargoyle item by producing the human-readable label and routing it to the engine's item-message routine so the player sees "gargoyle ".
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_SGARGL::look) — invoked when an actor/player examines or 'looks at' the gargoyle object.

function_map.name: U8P_SGARGL::look
function_map.role: Look/examine event handler for the gargoyle item; constructs display string and invokes messaging routine.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine to emit/display a short text/message associated with an item/process (used to show the object's label).

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the constructed label string ("gargoyle ") to the engine so the object produces a visible/audio message to the viewer.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the gargoyle object is examined (U8P_SGARGL::look event), the script builds the label string "gargoyle " and asks the item/process to 'bark' that string to the viewer.
